seo.title: Legal AI Email Assistant AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai email assistant AI agents in legal. Covers Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules, Court filing standards.

content.intro: Deploying an AI email assistant in a legal environment carries extraordinary risk — from malpractice exposure and bar sanctions to case dismissal from hallucinated citations, as demonstrated in Mata v. Avianca. This checklist ensures your AI email agent operates within attorney-client privilege protections, bar association ethics rules, and court-admissible standards before a single message is drafted or sent. Every item is scoped to the specific architecture of an email processing agent handling intent detection, draft generation, attachment handling, and send approval workflows.

sections.heading: Attorney Oversight & Approval Workflows
sections.description: Every AI-generated email draft must pass through a licensed attorney review gate before transmission. This section ensures no message leaves the system without human legal accountability.

items.label: Mandatory Attorney Review Gate
items.description: Implement a hard-stop approval workflow that requires a licensed attorney to explicitly approve every AI-generated draft before it is sent. The system must not allow auto-send under any configuration for client-facing emails.

items.label: Attorney Identity Verification on Approval
items.description: Require authenticated attorney sign-off (e.g., SSO with bar number validation) on each draft approval action. Log the approving attorney's name, bar number, timestamp, and IP address for audit trail purposes.

items.label: Draft Modification Tracking
items.description: Track all changes made by the reviewing attorney to the AI-generated draft using a diff log. Preserve both the original AI output and the final approved version to document the attorney's substantive contribution.

items.label: Escalation Routing for High-Stakes Emails
items.description: Configure intent detection to flag emails involving litigation deadlines, settlement offers, court filings, or opposing counsel communications for escalation to senior attorney review, not just any available reviewer.

items.label: Rejection and Feedback Loop
items.description: Provide attorneys with a structured rejection workflow that captures the reason for rejection (e.g., inaccurate citation, wrong tone, privilege concern). Feed rejection reasons into a quality monitoring dashboard, not back into model training.

items.label: Out-of-Scope Request Blocking
items.description: Define and enforce a list of email types the AI agent is prohibited from drafting entirely (e.g., legal opinions, engagement letters, court filings). Route these to attorneys with a clear handoff notification.

items.label: Audit Log Retention for Approved Emails
items.description: Retain complete audit logs of all AI-drafted, attorney-reviewed, and sent emails for a minimum of 7 years in a tamper-evident storage system, consistent with legal matter retention policies.

sections.heading: Citation Accuracy & Hallucination Prevention
sections.description: Hallucinated case citations are a documented, career-ending failure mode in legal AI (see Mata v. Avianca, 2023). This section enforces citation verification before any legal reference appears in a draft.

items.label: Real-Time Citation Verification Against Legal Databases
items.description: Integrate the draft generation pipeline with Westlaw, LexisNexis, or CourtListener APIs to verify every case citation before it is included in a draft. Block any citation that cannot be confirmed as a real, published case.

items.label: Citation Confidence Scoring with Threshold Enforcement
items.description: Assign a confidence score to each generated citation. Any citation below a defined threshold (e.g., 95% match confidence) must be automatically removed from the draft and flagged for attorney manual insertion.

items.label: Statute and Regulation Version Pinning
items.description: When referencing statutes or regulations, the system must pin the specific version and effective date (e.g., 'Fed. R. Civ. P. 26(b)(1) as amended Dec. 1, 2015'). Warn attorneys if a referenced rule has been amended since the pinned version.

items.label: Prohibition on Paraphrased or Reconstructed Citations
items.description: Explicitly block the model from generating paraphrased case names or reconstructed citations that approximate real cases. Only exact, database-verified citations may appear in output.

items.label: Citation Source Disclosure in Draft UI
items.description: Display the source database, retrieval timestamp, and full citation metadata alongside each citation in the attorney review interface so reviewers can independently verify before approving.

items.label: Negative Treatment Alerts
items.description: Check each cited case for negative treatment (overruled, distinguished, questioned) using Shepard's Citations or KeyCite and surface a warning to the reviewing attorney if the case has been negatively treated.

items.label: Hallucination Incident Logging
items.description: Log every instance where the model attempted to generate an unverifiable citation. Review these logs weekly to identify patterns and adjust retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) configurations accordingly.

sections.heading: Client Confidentiality & Data Governance
sections.description: Attorney-client privilege and client confidentiality are non-negotiable. This section governs how client data flows through the email agent, ensuring it is never exposed to model training pipelines or unauthorized parties.

items.label: Client Data Training Exclusion Policy
items.description: Implement a technical and contractual prohibition on using any client email content, matter details, or PII for model fine-tuning or training. Obtain written confirmation from your AI vendor that client data is excluded from training pipelines.

items.label: PII Detection and Redaction Before Processing
items.description: Run all inbound and outbound email content through a PII detection layer (e.g., Microsoft Presidio or AWS Comprehend) before it reaches the AI model. Automatically redact Social Security numbers, financial account numbers, and health information.

items.label: Matter-Scoped Data Isolation
items.description: Enforce strict data isolation so the AI agent can only access email threads, attachments, and context associated with the specific matter it is assisting on. Cross-matter data bleed must be architecturally prevented, not just policy-restricted.

items.label: Privilege Log Generation for AI-Touched Communications
items.description: Automatically generate privilege log entries for all emails drafted or processed by the AI agent, capturing date, author, recipient, subject matter, and privilege basis. Make these logs exportable for eDiscovery requests.

items.label: Third-Party Vendor Data Processing Agreement
items.description: Execute a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with your AI vendor that explicitly covers attorney-client privileged data, specifies data residency requirements, and prohibits subprocessor sharing without written consent.

items.label: Attachment Scanning for Confidential Document Markers
items.description: Scan all email attachments for confidentiality markers (e.g., 'PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL', 'ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT') before the AI agent processes or summarizes them. Flag marked documents for attorney-only handling.

items.label: Data Retention and Deletion Schedule
items.description: Define and enforce a retention schedule for AI-processed email data that aligns with matter closure timelines and applicable state bar rules. Implement automated deletion workflows with confirmation logging.

items.label: Breach Notification Workflow
items.description: Establish an automated alert and response workflow for potential privilege breaches (e.g., email sent to wrong recipient, unauthorized data access). Include client notification templates and bar reporting procedures.

sections.heading: Recipient Validation & Send Controls
sections.description: Sending a privileged communication to the wrong recipient is an immediate ethics violation. This section enforces strict recipient validation, tone controls, and send-gate logic specific to legal email workflows.

items.label: Opposing Counsel Direct Contact Detection
items.description: Cross-reference recipient email addresses against a represented-parties registry before drafting or sending. Block any draft addressed directly to a represented opposing party and route it to the supervising attorney with an ethics warning.

items.label: Recipient Domain Validation Against Matter Contacts
items.description: Validate that recipient email domains match known, matter-approved contacts before allowing send. Flag any recipient whose domain does not appear in the matter's contact list for attorney confirmation.

items.label: Reply-All Risk Screening
items.description: Detect reply-all scenarios where the recipient list includes both client contacts and opposing counsel or third parties. Require explicit attorney confirmation before allowing a reply-all send.

items.label: Tone and Professionalism Scoring
items.description: Run all AI-generated drafts through a tone classifier calibrated for legal professional standards. Flag drafts that score as aggressive, inflammatory, or unprofessional for attorney revision before approval.

items.label: Confidentiality Footer Enforcement
items.description: Automatically append a firm-approved attorney-client privilege and confidentiality footer to every outgoing email processed by the agent. Prevent the agent from removing or modifying this footer.

items.label: Deadline-Sensitive Email Prioritization and Delay Prevention
items.description: Detect emails referencing court deadlines, statute of limitations dates, or filing deadlines using date extraction. Flag these for immediate attorney review and implement a time-to-review SLA alert to prevent missed deadlines.

items.label: Attachment Verification Before Send
items.description: Verify that all attachments referenced in the email body are actually attached before the send gate opens. Block sends where the body references an attachment that is missing or where an attached document's metadata does not match the described content.

items.label: Send Confirmation Audit Trail
items.description: Record a complete send audit trail for every email including: AI draft timestamp, attorney review duration, approval action, final recipient list, and send timestamp. Store in the matter management system, not just email logs.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: ABA Model Rule 5.3 requires supervising attorneys to ensure that non-lawyer assistance — including AI tools — conforms to the professional obligations of the lawyer. Firms must document how attorney oversight is exercised over the AI email agent, including approval workflow records, to demonstrate compliance during bar audits or disciplinary proceedings.

Following Mata v. Avianca (S.D.N.Y. 2023), courts have imposed sanctions on attorneys who submitted AI-generated filings containing fabricated citations without verification. Any AI email agent that drafts communications referencing case law must integrate real-time citation verification against authoritative legal databases, and attorneys must personally confirm citation accuracy before any court-related communication is sent.

ABA Formal Opinion 477R and state bar equivalents require attorneys to use reasonable measures to prevent unauthorized access to client information transmitted electronically. For AI email agents, this means end-to-end encryption of email content in transit and at rest, vendor agreements prohibiting client data use for training, and documented security assessments of the AI platform before deployment.

Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26 and the Sedona Principles governing eDiscovery require that electronically stored information (ESI) processed by AI tools be producible and auditable. All AI-generated email drafts, attorney modifications, approval logs, and send records must be preserved in a format that supports litigation hold compliance and can be produced in response to eDiscovery requests without privilege waiver.

cta.heading: Automate Your Legal Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your Legal AI Email Assistant against these controls — catching privilege risks, citation failures, and approval gaps before they become malpractice.
